quizzes.title: Road Sign Recognition Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of common road signs including warning, regulatory, and informational signs used on public roads.
quizzes.instructions: Read each question carefully and select the correct sign meaning. Each question carries marks based on difficulty. There is no negative marking.

quizzes.title: Traffic Rules Basics Quiz
quizzes.description: A quiz covering essential traffic rules every driver must follow, including speed limits, lane discipline, and signal rules.
quizzes.instructions: Answer all questions based on standard traffic laws. Choose the single best answer for each question.

quizzes.title: Parking Rules Mastery Quiz
quizzes.description: Learn and test your understanding of legal and safe parking practices in different road situations.
quizzes.instructions: Select the most appropriate answer for each parking scenario described in the questions.

quizzes.title: Right of Way and Priority Rules Quiz
quizzes.description: Understand who has the right of way at intersections, roundabouts, and other priority situations on the road.
quizzes.instructions: Think about real driving situations while answering these priority and right-of-way questions.

quizzes.title: Driving Safety Essentials Quiz
quizzes.description: Test your knowledge of safe driving habits, hazard awareness, and accident prevention techniques.
quizzes.instructions: Choose the safest and most correct option for each question based on standard driving safety guidelines.

learningContents.title: Understanding Regulatory Road Signs
learningContents.subtitle: Signs that tell you what you must or must not do
learningContents.description: Learn how to identify and understand regulatory signs such as stop, yield, and no entry signs.
learningContents.content: Regulatory signs inform drivers of laws and regulations that must be obeyed. They are usually circular and have red borders for prohibitions or blue backgrounds for mandatory actions. Common examples include Stop, Give Way, No Entry, and Speed Limit signs. Ignoring these signs can lead to fines or accidents.

learningContents.title: Warning Signs on the Road
learningContents.subtitle: Triangular signs that alert you to hazards ahead
learningContents.description: A guide to recognizing warning signs like slippery road, narrow bridge, and pedestrian crossing signs.
learningContents.content: Warning signs are usually triangular with a red border and yellow or white background. They alert drivers to potential hazards such as sharp curves, slippery roads, or school zones ahead. Recognizing these signs early allows drivers to slow down and react safely.

learningContents.title: Mandatory and Informational Signs Explained
learningContents.subtitle: Blue signs that guide your driving direction
learningContents.description: A video explanation of mandatory direction signs and informational signs like parking and hospital signs.
learningContents.content: This video explains the difference between mandatory signs, which drivers must obey, and informational signs, which provide helpful guidance such as directions to hospitals, parking areas, or fuel stations.

learningContents.title: Quick Tips to Remember Road Signs
learningContents.subtitle: Simple memory tricks for new drivers
learningContents.description: Easy tips to help beginners remember the meaning of common road signs.
learningContents.content: Remember: red circles mean prohibition, triangles mean warning, and blue circles mean instruction. Practicing with flashcards and real road observation helps reinforce sign recognition over time.

learningContents.title: Speed Limit and School Zone Signs
learningContents.subtitle: Know your limits in sensitive areas
learningContents.description: Understand speed limit signs and special rules near schools and residential zones.
learningContents.content: Speed limit signs display the maximum legal speed for a road section. Near schools and residential areas, reduced speed limits are enforced during specific hours to protect pedestrians, especially children.

learningContents.title: Following Distance and Safe Speed
learningContents.subtitle: Why space matters between vehicles
learningContents.description: Learn the importance of maintaining a safe following distance based on speed and road conditions.
learningContents.content: Maintaining a safe following distance gives drivers enough time to react to sudden stops. The general rule is to keep at least a two-second gap in normal conditions, increasing this gap in rain, fog, or heavy traffic.

learningContents.title: Lane Discipline for Safe Driving
learningContents.subtitle: Stay in your lane, stay safe
learningContents.description: A guide to proper lane usage, overtaking rules, and lane markings.
learningContents.content: Lane discipline means staying within marked lanes, using indicators before changing lanes, and overtaking only from permitted sides. Poor lane discipline is a major cause of road accidents.

learningContents.title: Traffic Light Rules Explained
learningContents.subtitle: Red, yellow, and green explained simply
learningContents.description: A short video explaining how to respond correctly to traffic light signals.
learningContents.content: This video covers the meaning of red, yellow, and green traffic lights, along with special rules for flashing signals and turning during red lights where permitted.

learningContents.title: Avoiding Distracted Driving
learningContents.subtitle: Keep your focus on the road
learningContents.description: Simple tips to avoid mobile phone use and other distractions while driving.
learningContents.content: Avoid using your phone, eating, or adjusting devices while driving. Even a few seconds of distraction can lead to serious accidents. Use hands-free devices only when absolutely necessary.

learningContents.title: Seatbelt and Safety Equipment Laws
learningContents.subtitle: Buckle up every time
learningContents.description: Understand the legal requirements for seatbelts and safety equipment while driving.
learningContents.content: Wearing a seatbelt is mandatory for drivers and passengers in most countries. Seatbelts significantly reduce the risk of injury or death in the event of a collision.

learningContents.title: Where You Cannot Park
learningContents.subtitle: Understanding no-parking zones
learningContents.description: Learn about common no-parking zones such as near fire hydrants, crossings, and junctions.
learningContents.content: Parking is prohibited near fire hydrants, pedestrian crossings, bus stops, and road junctions. These restrictions ensure clear access for emergency vehicles and safe visibility for other road users.

learningContents.title: How to Parallel Park Safely
learningContents.subtitle: Step by step parallel parking guide
learningContents.description: A visual guide showing the correct technique for parallel parking.
learningContents.content: This video demonstrates the correct steps for parallel parking, including positioning, steering angles, and checking mirrors and blind spots before completing the maneuver.

learningContents.title: Parking on Hills and Slopes
learningContents.subtitle: Special precautions for uphill and downhill parking
learningContents.description: Learn how to safely park your vehicle on inclines to prevent rolling accidents.
learningContents.content: When parking on a hill, turn your wheels toward the curb if facing downhill, and away from the curb if facing uphill. Always apply the handbrake to prevent the vehicle from rolling.

learningContents.title: Disabled Parking Etiquette
learningContents.subtitle: Respecting reserved parking spaces
learningContents.description: Understand the rules around disabled parking permits and reserved spaces.
learningContents.content: Disabled parking spaces are reserved for vehicles displaying a valid permit. Parking in these spots without authorization is illegal and can result in fines.

learningContents.title: Night Parking Safety
learningContents.subtitle: Staying visible after dark
learningContents.description: Learn the rules for parking safely and visibly during nighttime hours.
learningContents.content: When parking at night, use parking lights if required by local law, and avoid parking on unlit roads or blind curves. Visibility is key to preventing collisions with parked vehicles.

learningContents.title: Right of Way at Uncontrolled Intersections
learningContents.subtitle: Who goes first when there are no signals
learningContents.description: Understand the standard rules for right of way at intersections without traffic lights or signs.
learningContents.content: At uncontrolled intersections, the general rule is to give way to traffic coming from the right, unless local laws specify otherwise. Always slow down and check all directions before proceeding.

learningContents.title: Roundabout Priority Rules
learningContents.subtitle: Navigating roundabouts safely
learningContents.description: A video explaining who has priority when entering and exiting a roundabout.
learningContents.content: This video explains that vehicles already inside a roundabout generally have priority over vehicles entering it. Drivers must yield and wait for a safe gap before joining.

learningContents.title: Priority for Emergency Vehicles
learningContents.subtitle: Always give way to sirens
learningContents.description: Learn the correct response when an emergency vehicle approaches with sirens or lights active.
learningContents.content: When an emergency vehicle approaches with sirens or flashing lights, drivers must safely move aside and allow it to pass. Never block or follow closely behind an emergency vehicle.

learningContents.title: Give Way Sign Explained
learningContents.subtitle: What to do when you see a give way sign
learningContents.description: A quick explanation of how to respond to give way and yield signs.
learningContents.content: A give way sign means you must slow down and let other traffic pass before proceeding. Stop completely if necessary to ensure it is safe to continue.

learningContents.title: Pedestrian Priority at Crossings
learningContents.subtitle: Respecting pedestrian right of way
learningContents.description: Understand when pedestrians have priority over vehicles at crossings.
learningContents.content: Pedestrians generally have the right of way at marked crossings, especially zebra crossings. Drivers must stop and allow pedestrians to cross safely before proceeding.

learningContents.title: The Importance of Seatbelts
learningContents.subtitle: Your first line of defense in a crash
learningContents.description: Learn why seatbelts are essential for driver and passenger safety.
learningContents.content: Seatbelts reduce the risk of serious injury or death in a crash by keeping occupants securely in place. Always ensure all passengers are buckled up before driving.

learningContents.title: Understanding Blind Spots
learningContents.subtitle: Areas your mirrors cannot show
learningContents.description: Learn how to identify and check blind spots before changing lanes or turning.
learningContents.content: Blind spots are areas around your vehicle not visible through mirrors. Always turn your head to check blind spots before changing lanes or merging into traffic.

learningContents.title: Driving Safely in Rain and Fog
learningContents.subtitle: Adjusting your driving for poor weather
learningContents.description: A video guide on safe driving techniques during rain, fog, and low visibility conditions.
learningContents.content: This video explains how to adjust speed, increase following distance, and use headlights properly when driving in rain, fog, or other low visibility conditions.

learningContents.title: Recognizing Driver Fatigue
learningContents.subtitle: Know the warning signs before it's too late
learningContents.description: Learn to recognize the signs of fatigue while driving and how to respond safely.
learningContents.content: Signs of driver fatigue include frequent yawning, difficulty focusing, and drifting between lanes. If you feel fatigued, pull over safely and rest before continuing your journey.

learningContents.title: Defensive Driving Basics
learningContents.subtitle: Anticipating hazards before they happen
learningContents.description: Learn the core principles of defensive driving to avoid accidents.
learningContents.content: Defensive driving involves anticipating potential hazards, maintaining safe distances, and staying alert to the actions of other road users. It helps reduce the risk of accidents caused by others' mistakes.

questions.questionText: What does a red circular sign with a white horizontal bar mean?
questions.explanationText: A red circle with a white horizontal bar is the standard No Entry sign, meaning vehicles are not allowed to pass in that direction.

questions.questionText: What shape is a standard Stop sign?
questions.explanationText: The Stop sign is uniquely octagon-shaped so it can be recognized even from behind or in poor visibility.

questions.questionText: What does a triangular sign with an exclamation mark usually indicate?
questions.explanationText: A triangular sign with an exclamation mark is a general warning sign alerting drivers to an unspecified hazard ahead.

questions.questionText: A sign showing children walking indicates what?

options.text: School zone or children crossing ahead

questions.explanationText: This sign warns drivers that they are approaching a school zone or an area where children may cross, requiring extra caution.

questions.questionText: What does a blue circular sign with a white arrow pointing left mean?

options.text: You may turn left if safe

questions.explanationText: Blue circular signs are mandatory instruction signs. An arrow pointing left means drivers must proceed in that direction.

questions.questionText: What does a circular sign with the number 60 and a red border mean?

options.text: Distance to next town is 60 km

questions.explanationText: A red-bordered circular sign with a number indicates the maximum legal speed limit for that road section.

questions.questionText: An inverted triangle sign usually represents which instruction?
questions.explanationText: An inverted (downward-pointing) triangle is internationally recognized as a Give Way or Yield sign.

questions.questionText: What does a blue square sign with a white 'P' usually indicate?
questions.explanationText: A blue square sign with a white 'P' is an informational sign indicating an available parking area.

questions.questionText: What does a circular sign with a red border and a bicycle symbol mean?

options.text: Shared path for cyclists and pedestrians

questions.explanationText: A red-bordered circle with a bicycle symbol prohibits cyclists from entering that road or path.

questions.questionText: A sign showing a curved arrow with a wavy line under a car usually warns of what?
questions.explanationText: This warning sign alerts drivers that the road surface ahead may be slippery, often due to rain, ice, or oil.

questions.questionText: What does a sign with a red circle and a car with a diagonal line through it mean?
questions.explanationText: A car symbol with a diagonal line inside a red circle means motor vehicles are prohibited from entering that road.

questions.questionText: What does a sign showing two arrows merging into one indicate?

options.text: Dual carriageway ends, lanes merge ahead

questions.explanationText: This sign warns drivers that two lanes are merging into one, requiring caution and possibly reduced speed.

questions.questionText: A rectangular sign showing a height in meters with a truck symbol indicates what?
questions.explanationText: This sign shows the maximum height a vehicle can be to safely pass under a bridge or structure ahead.

questions.questionText: What does a sign with a red circle containing a U-turn arrow crossed out mean?
questions.explanationText: A crossed-out U-turn arrow inside a red circle means drivers are not allowed to make a U-turn at that location.

questions.questionText: A sign showing a train symbol inside a triangle warns of what?
questions.explanationText: A train symbol in a warning triangle indicates an upcoming level crossing where a railway line crosses the road.

questions.questionText: What does a sign displaying 'Keep Left' with an arrow mean at a traffic island?

options.text: Drivers must pass on the left side of the island
Parking is allowed on the left

questions.explanationText: A Keep Left sign at a traffic island instructs drivers to pass the obstruction on its left-hand side.

questions.questionText: A sign showing a narrowing road with converging lines warns drivers of what?
questions.explanationText: Converging lines on a warning sign indicate that the road ahead becomes narrower, requiring drivers to slow down and be cautious.

questions.questionText: What does a sign showing a weight limit of 5 tonnes on a bridge mean?

options.text: Only vehicles under 5 tonnes may cross
Only vehicles over 5 tonnes may cross
The bridge is closed to all vehicles
Speed limit on the bridge is 5 km/h

questions.explanationText: A weight limit sign on a bridge means only vehicles with a total weight below the stated limit are permitted to cross safely.

questions.questionText: What does a sign showing a steep downward arrow with a percentage mean?
questions.explanationText: This sign indicates the steepness of a downhill slope ahead, warning drivers to control their speed using lower gears and brakes carefully.

questions.questionText: A sign with a red circle and the number 30 with a diagonal end line means what?

options.text: End of the 30 speed limit zone

questions.explanationText: A speed sign with a diagonal line through the number indicates the end of that specific speed limit zone.

questions.questionText: What should you do when approaching a school zone during school hours?

options.text: Reduce speed and watch for children

questions.explanationText: School zones require reduced speed during school hours to protect children who may cross unexpectedly.

questions.questionText: Who is required to wear a seatbelt in a moving vehicle?

options.text: All occupants where required by law

questions.explanationText: In most jurisdictions, all occupants, including rear passengers, are legally required to wear seatbelts while the vehicle is moving.

questions.questionText: What should you do when the traffic light turns yellow while you are approaching the intersection?

options.text: Speed up to cross before it turns red
Stop safely if it is possible to do so

questions.explanationText: A yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red. Drivers should stop safely if they can do so without sudden braking.

questions.questionText: From which side should you normally overtake another vehicle?
questions.explanationText: In most right-hand traffic countries, overtaking should be done from the right side unless road markings indicate otherwise.

questions.questionText: Is it legal to hold and use a mobile phone while driving?

options.text: No, unless using a hands-free device

questions.explanationText: Holding and using a mobile phone while driving is illegal in most regions unless a hands-free device is used, as it distracts the driver.

questions.questionText: What should you do before changing lanes?

options.text: Signal, check mirrors, and check blind spot

questions.explanationText: Before changing lanes, drivers must signal their intention, check mirrors, and check the blind spot to ensure it is safe to move.

questions.questionText: What is the safest blood alcohol level for driving?

options.text: As close to zero as possible

questions.explanationText: The safest approach is to avoid alcohol completely before driving, as even small amounts impair reaction time and judgment.

questions.questionText: When is it appropriate to use your vehicle horn?
questions.explanationText: The horn should only be used to alert other road users of potential danger, not for unnecessary noise or greetings.

questions.questionText: What should you do if you accidentally enter a one-way street from the wrong direction?

options.text: Stop, reverse carefully or find a safe way to exit
Turn on hazard lights and stay still

questions.explanationText: If you enter a one-way street the wrong way, you should stop, check for traffic, and carefully reverse or find a safe exit point.

questions.questionText: What is the recommended minimum following distance in normal driving conditions?
questions.explanationText: A minimum two-second gap allows enough time to react and stop safely if the vehicle ahead brakes suddenly.

questions.questionText: What should you do when an ambulance approaches from behind with its siren on?

options.text: Speed up to clear the way
Safely move aside and let it pass
Stop immediately in the middle of the road
Ignore it if you are in a hurry

questions.explanationText: When an emergency vehicle approaches with sirens active, drivers should safely move aside to allow it to pass without delay.

questions.questionText: When should you use your vehicle's headlights?

options.text: During night, fog, or low visibility conditions

questions.explanationText: Headlights must be used during night driving and whenever visibility is reduced, such as in fog, rain, or heavy dust.

questions.questionText: What must you do before making a right turn at an intersection?

options.text: Signal, check traffic, and yield if required
Stop completely even with a green light
Use the horn instead of signaling

questions.explanationText: Before turning right, drivers must signal their intention, check for oncoming traffic and pedestrians, and yield if necessary.

questions.questionText: Which lane should slower vehicles generally use on a multi-lane motorway?
questions.explanationText: Slower vehicles should stay in the leftmost lane, keeping the right lanes free for overtaking traffic.

questions.questionText: Why is tailgating considered dangerous?

options.text: It reduces reaction time in emergencies
It is required in heavy traffic

questions.explanationText: Tailgating, or following too closely, reduces the time a driver has to react if the vehicle ahead suddenly brakes, increasing collision risk.

questions.questionText: At a zebra crossing with a pedestrian waiting to cross, what must a driver do?

options.text: Speed up to pass before they step out
Stop and allow the pedestrian to cross
Only stop if a police officer is present

questions.explanationText: Drivers must stop and give way to pedestrians waiting at or crossing a zebra crossing, as pedestrians have priority there.

questions.questionText: When entering a roundabout, which vehicles generally have priority?
questions.explanationText: Vehicles already circulating inside a roundabout generally have priority over vehicles waiting to enter.

questions.questionText: How early should you signal before turning at an intersection?

options.text: Well in advance to alert other drivers
Only if other vehicles are nearby

questions.explanationText: Signaling well in advance gives other road users enough time to react and understand your intended movement.

questions.questionText: When is overtaking considered unsafe and prohibited?

options.text: Near a bend, junction, or when visibility is poor
On a motorway with clear lanes

questions.explanationText: Overtaking is unsafe near bends, junctions, or in poor visibility because oncoming traffic or hazards may not be seen in time.

questions.questionText: Is it legal to park directly in front of a fire hydrant?

options.text: Yes, if only for a few minutes
Yes, if hazard lights are on

questions.explanationText: Parking near a fire hydrant is illegal because it can block emergency access needed to fight fires quickly.

questions.questionText: Who is allowed to park in a disabled parking space?

options.text: Only vehicles with a valid disabled permit

questions.explanationText: Disabled parking spaces are reserved exclusively for vehicles displaying a valid disability parking permit.

questions.questionText: Can you park your vehicle on a pedestrian crossing?

options.text: No, it is illegal and dangerous

questions.explanationText: Parking on a pedestrian crossing is illegal because it blocks pedestrian visibility and safe passage.

questions.questionText: What should you do when parking on an unlit road at night?

options.text: Keep parking lights on if required by law

questions.explanationText: On unlit roads, parking lights should be kept on where required so the vehicle remains visible to other road users at night.

questions.questionText: What is a loading zone reserved for?
questions.explanationText: Loading zones are reserved for vehicles actively loading or unloading goods and should not be used for regular parking.

questions.questionText: Which way should you turn your wheels when parking facing downhill?
questions.explanationText: When facing downhill, turning wheels toward the curb ensures the vehicle rolls into the curb if the brakes fail, preventing it from rolling into traffic.

questions.questionText: How close to a road junction is it generally unsafe to park?

options.text: Right at the junction is fine
Within a specified minimum distance, often several meters

questions.explanationText: Parking too close to a junction blocks visibility for turning vehicles and pedestrians, so a minimum safe distance is required by law.

questions.questionText: What is an advantage of reverse parking into a space?

options.text: It gives better visibility when exiting the space later
It is required in all car parks

questions.explanationText: Reverse parking allows a driver to exit the space by driving forward, which usually provides better visibility and safety.

questions.questionText: What is double parking, and why is it discouraged?

options.text: Parking in two spaces at once, it saves space
Parking alongside another parked vehicle, blocking traffic flow
Parking twice in the same day
Parking with two drivers in the car

questions.explanationText: Double parking means stopping alongside an already parked vehicle, which blocks traffic flow and can cause accidents.

questions.questionText: Why is parking near a bus stop typically restricted?

options.text: It is only a suggestion, not a rule
It blocks buses from stopping safely for passengers
Buses do not need dedicated stopping space

questions.explanationText: Parking restrictions near bus stops ensure buses can safely pull in and out to pick up and drop off passengers.

questions.questionText: Is it legal to park a vehicle on a footpath or sidewalk?

options.text: Yes, if the road is narrow

questions.explanationText: Parking on a footpath is illegal in most areas because it obstructs pedestrians, especially those with strollers or wheelchairs.

questions.questionText: What is important to check before pulling out of an angle parking space?

options.text: Only the front of the vehicle
Mirrors, blind spots, and passing traffic

questions.explanationText: Before leaving an angle parking space, drivers should check mirrors and blind spots carefully since visibility of passing traffic can be limited.

questions.questionText: When should you use hazard lights while parked?

options.text: When temporarily stopped in a way that may surprise other drivers

questions.explanationText: Hazard lights should be used when a vehicle is stopped unexpectedly, such as a breakdown, to warn other drivers of the obstruction.

questions.questionText: What typically happens if you repeatedly violate parking regulations?

options.text: Fines, towing, or penalty points depending on local law
A warning letter only, with no further action

questions.explanationText: Repeated parking violations can result in fines, vehicle towing, or penalty points depending on local traffic regulations.

questions.questionText: Why is parking on a sharp curve considered dangerous?

options.text: It improves visibility for other drivers
It reduces visibility and reaction time for approaching drivers
It is required by law in some areas
It has no effect on safety

questions.explanationText: Parking on a curve reduces the visibility and reaction time for approaching drivers, increasing the risk of collision.

questions.questionText: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs, who usually has the right of way?

options.text: Traffic coming from the right, in most countries

questions.explanationText: In most right-hand traffic countries, vehicles must give way to traffic approaching from the right at uncontrolled intersections.

questions.questionText: What must you do when you see a Give Way sign?

options.text: Slow down and yield to other traffic
Stop only if a pedestrian is present

questions.explanationText: A Give Way sign requires drivers to slow down and let other traffic pass safely before proceeding.

questions.questionText: Who has priority at a marked zebra crossing?

options.text: Pedestrians crossing or about to cross

questions.explanationText: Pedestrians at a marked zebra crossing generally have priority, and vehicles must stop to let them cross safely.

questions.questionText: Do emergency vehicles with sirens on have priority over regular traffic?

options.text: No, they must follow normal rules
Yes, other vehicles must give way

questions.explanationText: Emergency vehicles with active sirens and lights have priority, and other drivers must safely give way to let them pass.

questions.questionText: When entering a roundabout, what should you do if a vehicle is already circulating inside?

options.text: Enter immediately regardless of the other vehicle
Wait until it is safe and give way to circulating traffic

questions.explanationText: Vehicles entering a roundabout must give way to traffic already circulating inside it before joining safely.

questions.questionText: At a T-junction, which traffic generally has priority?

options.text: Traffic on the continuing main road

questions.explanationText: At a T-junction, traffic on the continuing main road generally has priority over traffic joining from the terminating road.

questions.questionText: When two lanes merge into one and no signs are present, what is the safest approach?

options.text: Force your way in front of other vehicles
Use a zipper merge, alternating turns fairly
Drive on the shoulder to bypass merging

questions.explanationText: A zipper merge, where vehicles from each lane alternate smoothly, is the safest and most efficient way to merge without signs.

questions.questionText: At a narrow bridge where only one vehicle can pass, who typically has priority?

options.text: The vehicle already on the bridge or closer to it

questions.explanationText: At narrow bridges, the vehicle that is already on the bridge or closest to entering it typically has priority to cross first.

questions.questionText: When turning right across a bike lane, what must a driver do regarding cyclists?

options.text: Give way to cyclists traveling straight through
Only give way if the cyclist honks

questions.explanationText: Drivers turning across a bike lane must give way to cyclists who are continuing straight through the intersection.

questions.questionText: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign with a black border typically indicate about priority?

options.text: You are on a priority road

questions.explanationText: A yellow diamond priority sign indicates that the road you are on has priority over side roads joining it.

questions.questionText: At a four-way stop intersection, who should proceed first?
questions.explanationText: At a four-way stop, the vehicle that arrives first generally has the right to proceed first, and ties are resolved by yielding to the right.

questions.questionText: What should drivers do at an intersection where the traffic light is not working?

options.text: Treat it as an uncontrolled intersection and apply right-of-way rules
Wait indefinitely until it is repaired
Only the first vehicle to arrive can pass without stopping

questions.explanationText: When traffic lights are not working, drivers should treat the intersection as uncontrolled and follow standard right-of-way rules, stopping and yielding as needed.

questions.questionText: Why should you always wear a seatbelt while driving?

options.text: It reduces the risk of serious injury in a crash
It is not necessary for short trips
It only helps at high speed

questions.explanationText: Seatbelts keep occupants securely in place during a collision, significantly reducing the risk of serious injury or death.

questions.questionText: What should you do before changing lanes to avoid a blind spot collision?

options.text: Turn your head to check the blind spot
Use the horn instead of checking

questions.explanationText: Mirrors do not show everything, so turning your head to check the blind spot is essential before changing lanes safely.

questions.questionText: What should you do if you start feeling drowsy while driving?

options.text: Increase speed to reach your destination faster
Open the window and continue driving
Play loud music and keep going

questions.explanationText: If you feel drowsy, the safest action is to pull over in a safe location and rest before continuing your journey.

questions.questionText: How often should you check your mirrors while driving?

options.text: Only at the start of the trip

questions.explanationText: Regularly checking mirrors throughout your drive helps you stay aware of surrounding traffic and react to changing conditions.

questions.questionText: What is required for transporting young children safely in a vehicle?

options.text: Holding the child on your lap
Letting the child sit in the front seat unrestrained

questions.explanationText: Young children must be secured in an appropriate child safety seat suited to their age and size to protect them in a crash.

questions.questionText: How should you adjust your driving in heavy rain?

options.text: Reduce speed and increase following distance
Drive closer to the vehicle ahead for guidance

questions.explanationText: In heavy rain, reducing speed and increasing following distance helps prevent skidding and allows more time to react to hazards.

questions.questionText: What is an important safety practice when driving at night?

options.text: Use high beams at all times, even with oncoming traffic
Dim headlights for oncoming vehicles and reduce speed
Drive without headlights to save battery
Increase speed since roads are emptier

questions.explanationText: At night, drivers should dim their headlights for oncoming traffic to avoid blinding other drivers and reduce speed for safer reaction time.

questions.questionText: What should you do if you see an animal crossing sign on a rural road?

options.text: Slow down and stay alert for animals crossing
Speed up to pass the area quickly

questions.explanationText: Animal crossing signs warn drivers to slow down and stay alert, as animals may suddenly cross the road in that area.

questions.questionText: Why is it important to regularly check tire pressure and tread?

options.text: It has no effect on safety
Proper tires improve grip and reduce skidding risk
It is only necessary once a year

questions.explanationText: Well-maintained tires with correct pressure and tread depth improve road grip and significantly reduce the risk of skidding or blowouts.

questions.questionText: What lights should you use when driving in thick fog?

options.text: Fog lights and low beam headlights

questions.explanationText: In fog, low beam headlights and fog lights improve visibility without reflecting light back and blinding the driver, unlike high beams.

questions.questionText: What is the safest way to handle an incoming phone call while driving?

options.text: Answer and talk while holding the phone
Let it go to voicemail or use hands-free if necessary
Look at the phone screen while driving

questions.explanationText: The safest option is to let calls go to voicemail or use a hands-free system only when truly necessary, keeping full attention on the road.

questions.questionText: What should you do if your vehicle begins to hydroplane on a wet road?

options.text: Ease off the accelerator and steer straight

questions.explanationText: During hydroplaning, easing off the accelerator and keeping the steering wheel straight helps the tires regain contact with the road safely.

questions.questionText: What is the correct technique for an emergency stop in a modern vehicle with ABS?

options.text: Apply firm, continuous pressure to the brake pedal
Release the brake immediately after pressing

questions.explanationText: With ABS, drivers should apply firm and continuous pressure to the brake pedal, allowing the system to prevent wheel lock-up automatically.

questions.questionText: What is a key principle of defensive driving?

options.text: Assume other drivers will always follow the rules
Anticipate potential hazards and react early
Focus only on your own vehicle's speed
React only after a hazard occurs

questions.explanationText: Defensive driving means anticipating potential hazards from other road users and reacting early to avoid accidents.

questions.questionText: What should you do first if your brakes suddenly fail while driving?

options.text: Shift to a lower gear and use the handbrake gradually
Jump out of the moving vehicle
Accelerate to find a safe stopping area

questions.explanationText: If brakes fail, shifting to a lower gear and gradually applying the handbrake helps slow the vehicle safely without locking the wheels suddenly.
